claim_summary: Chapter 5 is a chronology reconstruction using verified, partially verified, and unable-to-verify chronology categories without hypothesis evaluation.
notes: No motive, cause, or identity inference.

claim_summary: The chapter reconstructs events from chronology_master.csv, chronology_uncertainties.csv, and event_source_map.csv for orientation rather than explanation.
The admitted chronology contains 24 events: 11 verified, 11 partially verified, 2 unable-to-verify later placeholders, and 8 removed or unsupported timeline claims logged as uncertainties.
Dates and times are presented only at the precision supported by the source; gaps are not filled by inference.
The chronology verification summary table reports verified, partially verified, unable-to-verify, and removed/unsupported event counts.
No verified or partially verified pre-1948 event is admitted into the master chronology.
notes: Does not deny historical biography; only states repository admission status.

claim_summary: The first admitted chronology entries begin on 30 November 1948 with transport-ticket evidence linked to property found on or with the body.
Townsend was on duty from 6:15 a.m. to about 2:00 p.m.; the Henley Beach ticket was issued that day, but precise time and purchaser identity are unknown.
The bus ticket is tied to the Adelaide-Somerton route departing about 11:15 a.m., but holder, boarding point, and alighting point remain uncertain.
At 7:00 p.m. on 30 November a man was seen lying where the body was later found and raising his right arm; identity continuity was not proven by facial identification.
At about 7:30 p.m. a man and girl saw a man in the same place with no observed movement; this does not establish certainty that the person was the deceased.
The absence of intervening witnesses between bus arrival and the 7:00 p.m. sighting is negative witness evidence, not proof of absence.
At about 7:00 a.m. on 1 December 1948 the body of an unknown man was found on the shore at Somerton Beach.
Dr Bennett examined the body at about 9:40 a.m. and estimated death about eight hours earlier; medical time-of-death entries are approximate.
The body was taken to the City Mortuary, but the exact transfer time is not established in the extracted chronology.
The initial clothing search found transport tickets and small property, and the Tamam Shud slip was not found in that initial search.
Constable John Moss is indexed as a first responding police witness for body position, property search, visible condition, and transport details.
notes: Witness reliability reserved for later chapter.

claim_summary: The chronology does not admit a solved identification event for 1948 or 1949.
On 2 December 1948 Cowan received stomach contents, liver and muscle, urine, and blood from the unidentified man.
Cowan was unable to find signs of common poison in submitted specimens; this is not evidence that all poisons were absent.
The post-mortem chronology establishes specimen receipt and negative common-poison testing only, not exact cause of death or absence of all toxic agents.
The Tamam Shud slip was found later in a difficult-to-locate trouser fob pocket; exact date, time, and finder identity remain unresolved.
The Tamam Shud paper was associated by the coroner with a second edition Fitzgerald Rubaiyat; book recovery chronology and provenance remain unresolved.
An unclaimed suitcase was found at Adelaide Railway Station luggage office a little over a month after 1 December 1948; exact date and custody remain gaps.
The suitcase was assessed as internally connected by clothing size, removed tags, and thread similarity, but ownership and complete custody are not established.
Public appeals and interstate inquiries are described only where source-mapped event records exist.
The major investigative milestones table summarizes selected CHR events from discovery through inquest opening.
The 1949 inquest period begins on 17 June 1949 and DOC-0001 contains proceedings and remarks concerning the Somerton death.
The inquest was adjourned sine die after recording the deceased as unknown, death as not natural, and precise circumstances and cause as not stated with certainty.
The 1949 inquest witness set includes medical, police, transport, railway, beach, forensic, and exhibit witnesses, but reliability assessment is reserved for the testimony chapter.
GRG 1/27 File 53/1958 is identified as a later coronial file dated 14 March 1958, but content review and original-custodian access remain limited.
Archival handling and a 14 May 2014 certified death-certificate copy are documentation-preservation events, not original death events.
The 2021 exhumation is retained only as an unable-to-verify placeholder because the official primary release was not acquired and reviewed for Mission 4.
verification_status: Unable to verify in primary archive

claim_summary: The 2022-2026 Webb identity and genetic-genealogy chronology is retained only as an unable-to-verify later placeholder and not treated as an officially verified identity event.
verification_status: Unable to verify in primary archive

claim_summary: The master timeline is included from the Mission 5 LaTeX table with event IDs, dates, locations, summaries, sources, and verification status.

The strongest chronology anchors are discovery, transport-ticket evidence, evening sightings, initial search, mortuary transfer, specimen receipt, negative common-poison analysis, Tamam Shud discovery, suitcase lead, and inquest proceedings.
notes: Summarizes verified and high-confidence partial events.

claim_summary: Partially verified chronology prevents overstatement of death timing, search timing, Tamam Shud discovery date, Rubaiyat provenance, suitcase custody, and 1958 file status.

The largest chronology gaps concern ticket use, evening-sighting identity continuity, exact death time/place, search timing, Rubaiyat recovery, suitcase custody, and modern official-status documentation.
The uncertainty table groups the major chronology uncertainties by evidence area and related CHR records.
The chronology supports disciplined sequencing of events, but not a completed explanation.
